title: Jafar Panahi's Latest Drama Earns Four Golden Globe Nominations
text: Iranian filmmaker Jafar Panahi's 'It Was Just an Accident' earned four Golden Globe nominations at the 2026 ceremony. The film received nominations for Best Picture (Drama), Best Director, Best Screenplay, and Best Foreign Language Film. This achievement is significant for Iranian cinema's representation at major international awards. The film had its world premiere at the main competition of the 78th Cannes Film Festival on May 20, 2025, where it won the Palme d'Or. At the 83rd Golden Globe Awards, it became the first Iranian film to be nominated for Best Motion Picture – Drama, Best Director and Best Screenplay. About the director, Panahi is critical of the Iranian government and has been imprisoned several times. He made the film without official filming permission from the Iranian authorities. Director Jafar Panahi is currently sentenced to one year in prison, a two-year travel ban, and a prohibition on joining political or social organizations or factions through a trial in absentia for propaganda activities against the Iranian regime.
